content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 5, 1994


Gail Martini
Tri-County Apartment Assn.
792 Meridian Way, Suite A
San Jose, CA  95126

                                        Re:  Your Request for Confirmation
                                             of Telephone Advice
                                             Our File No. I-94-170

Dear Ms. Martini:

     This is to confirm telephone assistance provided to you on
June 13, 1994, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the Act"). (Government Code Sections
81000-91015.)

     If the Association holds a joint fundraiser for Tri-PAC and
Tri-Issues PAC, it would need to advise the contributors of the
specific amount allocated to each committee.

     The Act does not prohibit the Association from consolidating
the Tri-PAC and Tri-Issues PAC. In addition, there would be no
restrictions under the Act on the consolidated PAC's ability to
contribute to both candidates and issues.

     The Act does not address tax issues affecting committees,
whether a committee should be set up as a corporation, or how a
committee would operate as a corporation.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Colleen McGee
                                        Political Reform Consultant
                                        Technical Assistance Division


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA  95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: May an association hold a joint fundraiser for multiple PACs, and may those PACs be consolidated under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Joint fundraisers for multiple PACs are permissible if contributors are advised of specific allocations to each committee. PACs may be consolidated with no restrictions on contributing to both candidates and issues. Tax and corporate structure matters are outside FPPC jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Confirmation letter of prior telephone advice; no formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present; content extracted from letter body; tax and corporate structure matters explicitly noted as outside FPPC scope

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May an association hold a joint fundraiser for multiple PACs, and may those PACs be consolidated under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: Joint fundraisers for multiple PACs are permissible if contributors are advised of specific allocations to each committee. PACs may be consolidated with no restrictions on contributing to both candidates and issues. Tax and corporate structure matters are outside FPPC jurisdiction.

(Government Code Sections 81000-91015.) If the Association holds a joint fundraiser for Tri-PAC and Tri-Issues PAC, it would need to advise the contributors of the specific amount allocated to each committee. The Act does not prohibit the Association from consolidating the Tri-PAC and Tri-Issues PAC. In addition, there would be no restrictions under the Act on the consolidated PAC's ability to contribute to both candidates and issues. The Act does not address tax issues affecting committees, whether a committee should be set up as a corporation, or how a committee would operate as a corporation. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5662. Sincerely, [signature] Colleen McGee Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding campaign disclosure provisions, advising that an association may hold joint fundraisers for multiple PACs (with contributor notification requirements) and may consolidate PACs without restrictions on their contribution abilities.
